kill_fact: No kill_fact breaks the bear thesis. Every load-bearing figure verifies against a primary source, and the bear case (great business, fully-priced entry; scale-out conviction should be capped LOW) survives all four attack vectors. The one correction worth flagging is on the BULL side, not the bear's: the debate already states the right number (US$66.21/lb realized) but live data shows EV/EBITDA is ~76x (stockanalysis, Jun-2026), even richer than the ~50x the debate used — so the bear's 'fully-priced / re-rate spent' verdict is, if anything, understated, not refuted.
corrections: (1) Realized-price clarity: the Q1-2026 realized uranium price is US$66.21/lb = CA$91.26/lb. The debate's US$66.21/lb is CORRECT; some third-party summaries (StockTitan) mislabel the CA$91.26 figure as USD — do not let that propagate as a US$91 print. (2) Valuation is RICHER than stated: live EV/EBITDA ~76x (stockanalysis, Jun-2026) vs the debate's ~50x; fwd P/E ~98x and GF Value ~$67 both corroborate the overvaluation. The bear's downside-to-defensible-value is therefore at least as large as claimed. (3) sop_fair_value (~$77) is built on CURRENT Q1-2026 (reported 2026-05-04/05) + FY2025 financials — NOT stale; the stale-anchor attack fails. (4) US-framework terms verify exactly: ≥$80B AP1000 build, US-Gov 20% of Westinghouse distributions above US$17.5B, vesting gated on FID + definitive agreements that do NOT yet exist (none signed Jun-2026) — confirming the catalyst is FIRED with a value-leaking, pre-FID tail, not an imminent up-trigger.
evidence: Cameco Reports First Quarter 2026 Results (cameco.com / BusinessWire, 2026-05-04): revenue $845M (+7%), net earnings $131M (+87%), adj EBITDA $509M (+44%), average realized uranium price US$66.21/lb (CA$91.26/lb) — confirms the bull/bear hard numbers and the realized-price clarification.

US Government / Westinghouse / Cameco / Brookfield $80B AP1000 partnership (K&L Gates HUB + Utility Dive, announced 2025-10-28): ≥US$80B of new AP1000 reactors; US-Gov Participation Interest = 20% of Westinghouse cash distributions above US$17.5B; vesting REQUIRES a final investment decision + definitive agreements for ≥$80B that do not yet exist — confirms catalyst FIRED + structural value leakage + pre-FID gate.

Cameco (CCJ) Statistics & Valuation (stockanalysis.com, Jun-2026): trailing P/E ~114x, forward P/E ~98x, EV/EBITDA ~76x; GuruFocus GF Value ~$67 vs ~$107 price — corroborates 'fully-priced, easy money made' and makes the bear's downside case if anything more conservative than reality.

Cameco 2026 Q1 6-K / Quarterly Report (SEC EDGAR data/0001009001, filed May-2026): Westinghouse still a GAAP equity LOSS to Cameco while adj EBITDA grows; production guidance held (19.5-21.5 Mlbs Cameco-share) — confirms the 'mixed up-cycle' and 'discipline-not-hype' bear/interrogator framing.
